claims.claim_text: The Knight Capital 2012 incident is a canonical example of a partial rollout and inadequate release controls causing a production availability crisis.
claims.span_hint: section: Why This Module Matters, first paragraph; repeated in Did You Know, third bullet
claims.rationale: The SEC primary source directly documents the 2012 Knight Capital deployment/control failure, but `sec.gov` is off the current allowlist.

claims.claim_text: Ephemeral containers are useful when `kubectl exec` is insufficient because a container has crashed or the image lacks debugging utilities, and `kubectl debug` can add an ephemeral container to a running Pod.
claims.span_hint: section: Cloud-Native Debugging With AI and Kubernetes, paragraphs around the distroless-container discussion and `k debug` example
claims.rationale: The Kubernetes task guide explicitly states these ephemeral-container use cases and shows `kubectl debug` adding one to a running Pod.

claims.claim_text: `kubectl top` depends on Metrics Server and is intended as a recent resource-usage view rather than a high-accuracy profiler.
claims.span_hint: section: Optimization: Profile First, Then Ask the Model, paragraph beginning "Performance work in Kubernetes adds another layer"
claims.rationale: The official `kubectl top` reference says it fetches metrics from Metrics Server and is optimized for autoscaling signals rather than pinpoint accuracy.

claims.claim_text: `kubectl top pod` output may be unavailable for a few minutes after Pod creation because of metrics pipeline delay.
claims.span_hint: section: Cloud-Native Debugging With AI and Kubernetes, paragraph beginning "Kubernetes metrics need interpretation as well."; also Common Mistakes table row about missing Kubernetes metrics
claims.rationale: The official `kubectl top pod` reference explicitly notes that metrics may be unavailable for a few minutes since Pod creation.

claims.claim_text: Kubernetes v1.35+ supports structured `/statusz` and `/flagz` responses, and in v1.36 these structured responses are beta and intended for machine-parseable debugging/introspection tools.
claims.span_hint: section: Cloud-Native Debugging With AI and Kubernetes, paragraph beginning "Control-plane and node debugging require additional skepticism."
claims.rationale: The Kubernetes z-pages reference directly states the v1.35 structured-response support and the v1.36 beta machine-parseable intent.

claims.claim_text: The Harvard Mark II moth anecdote helped popularize the term "debugging" in computing culture, though the term itself predates that event.
claims.span_hint: section: Did You Know?, first bullet
claims.rationale: The allowlisted Wikipedia article directly covers the Mark II moth anecdote and notes earlier pre-computing uses of the term.

claims.claim_text: The Therac-25 accidents showed that software defects combined with weak safety engineering can cause catastrophic real-world harm.
claims.span_hint: section: Did You Know?, second bullet
claims.rationale: The ACM article explicitly describes the Therac-25 accidents as fatal overdoses and frames the lesson as compounded design/safety-engineering failure.

claims.claim_text: The Mars Climate Orbiter loss is a classic unit-mismatch/interface-contract failure.
claims.span_hint: section: Did You Know?, fourth bullet
claims.rationale: NASA directly documents the English-versus-metric mismatch and verification/validation lesson, but `nasa.gov` is off the current allowlist.

further_reading.title: Debug Running Pods
further_reading.why_relevant: Official Kubernetes guidance for logs, `exec`, ephemeral containers, and copied-Pod debugging workflows.

further_reading.title: Kubernetes z-pages
further_reading.why_relevant: Explains the structured `/statusz` and `/flagz` endpoints that make control-plane diagnostics easier to inspect programmatically.

further_reading.title: What Have We Learned About Software Engineering?
further_reading.why_relevant: Provides concise incident-driven lessons from Therac-25 and other failures that reinforce the module’s debugging discipline.

notes: Only non-pedagogical factual claims that name real incidents or specific Kubernetes capabilities were seeded; examples, analogies, exercises, and generic debugging advice were intentionally omitted.
